title: Lead
description: One artificial-turf lead. Two tiers: the flat core (what a BDM sees; maps 1:1 to a Supabase 'lead' row) plus an optional nested 'evidence' block holding the richer extraction detail. Version 2.0: replaces the Salesforce-shaped v1 record; lifecycle fields (status, assigned_bdm, change log) live in Supabase, not in this file contract. See docs/SCHEMAS.md.

external_id.description: Stable, content-derived id: hash of normalized organization + project label + primary source URL. Unchanged recipe from v1 so ids stay stable across the version bump. Dedup/upsert key everywhere (ledger, Supabase).

source.description: Which pipeline surfaced this lead. This repo always writes 'web-search'; the meeting-minutes repo writes 'meeting-minutes'. Kept on every lead so the trial can compare the two pipelines.

organization.description: Name of the entity the lead is about (school district, city, county).

organization_id.description: Slug key into organizations/registry.json (e.g. leander-isd-tx). Optional but strongly preferred; links the lead to the org's geography.

state.description: Two-letter US state of the organization.

county.description: The organization's PRIMARY county. Districts can span counties; the full coverage set lives on the organization record, not here. Empty string only when genuinely unresolved.

summary.description: One-line, plain-language summary a salesperson can read at a glance: what the project is, which sports, and where it stands.

evidence_quote.description: The single best verbatim quote from the source that supports the lead (~400 chars max in practice). Empty string if the source is not quotable (e.g. a bid-portal table).

source_url.description: One deep link to the page the lead was found on, so a BDM can open the source and validate/research the lead. Required and non-empty.

discovered_at.description: UTC timestamp when the pipeline first recorded this lead.

location_id.description: Which search area (locations/registry.yaml) surfaced this lead. Search input, not lead geography.

evidence.description: Optional detail tier: everything the agent extracted beyond the core lead. Never shown in the default BDM view; available on demand.

project_name.description: Label: [Site or facility name] - [compact scope]. 3-8 words. Input to the external_id hash.

project_address.description: Project site address if resolved. Empty string otherwise.

projected_start_date.description: Expected start (ISO or free text). Empty string if unknown.

details.description: Full extracted narrative: scope, sports, funding, dollar amounts, caveats.

contact.description: Point of contact, only if stated on the source. Unknown fields are empty strings.

source_ids.description: All source ids this lead traces back to (the core carries only the one best URL).

source_urls.description: All page URLs the lead was found on.

matched_terms.description: Keyword-filter terms that qualified the lead (from config/keyword_filters.yaml).

confidence.description: Extraction confidence. Below threshold routes the lead to human review.

needs_review.description: True if a core field is missing, the sport is unconfirmed, or confidence is below threshold.

run_timestamp.description: Run folder (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ) of the scrape that produced this lead.
